TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN JOANNA DAMAGING A CAR WASH AND FIRE STATION GARAGE DOOR. THE TORNADO TRAVELED THROUGH HAWKINS CIRCLE AND RENNO ROAD WHERE THE INTENSITY INCREASED TO EF1 RESULTING IN NUMEROUS LARGE TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED, SOME DOWNED ON HOUSES. AS THE STORM APPROACHED AND CROSSED HIGHWAY 72, DOWNING ADDITIONAL LARGE TREES, SOME BLOCKING THE HIGHWAY, BEFORE DISSIPATING IN THE SUMTER NATIONAL FOREST.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR OLD WILLIS SCHOOL ROAD AND TRACKED EAST, CROSSING HIGHWAY 27 ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF STANLEY. THE STORM CONTINUED MOVING EAST, EVENTUALLY DISSIPATING JUST EAST OF STANLEY-LUCIA ROAD. SCATTERED TREE AND LIMB DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED ALONG THE TORNADOS PATH WITH MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 75 MPH.

TORNADO DAMAGE STARTED AT DEADFALL ROAD W WHERE SCATTERED LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. THE TORNADO THEN INTENSIFIED INTO A LOW-END EF1 AS IT CROSSED COKESBURY RD INTO DEADFALL ROAD E WHERE NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED, INCLUDING DOWNED TREES ON MULTIPLE HOMES. EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO TWO HOUSES WERE FOUND AT THE SURVEY. THE TORNADO WEAKENED AS IT APPROACHED THE GREENWOOD AIRPORT CAUSING MORE TREE DAMAGE TO A FEW NEIGHBORHOODS. THE GREENWOOD AIRPORT REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 75MPH IN LINE WITH THE TORNADO. THE MOST INTENSE DAMAGE WAS IN A NARROW AND SLIGHTLY CONVERGENT LINE WITH AN AREA OF ROTATION ON RADAR ALOFT AT THE SAME TIME AND LOCATION.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTHERN LAURENS COUNTY AND SOUTHERN SPARTANBURG COUNTY, DOWNING NUMEROUS TREES IN ITS PATH WHILE STARTING WEST OF GRAY COURT. THERE WAS EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE ON BARNYARD CT, WHERE MULTIPLE TREES FELL ON TOP OF A HOUSE, CAUSING EF-1 RATED DAMAGE. THE TORNADO THEN TRAVELED INTO DOWNTOWN GRAY COURT WHERE SPORADIC SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES FOLLOWED A NARROW PATH. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO TRAVEL THROUGH BLACKBERRY RD AND IN THE VICINITY OF HIGHWAY 92. THE TORNADO THEN TRAVELED ACROSS ORA RD, WHILE UPROOTING OR SNAPPING AT LEAST A DOZEN TREES NEAR A POND ON THE INTERSECTION OF GARRET RD AND ORA RD. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED BETWEEN MM41 AND MM42 ON INTERSTATE 26, NEAR ENOREE. THE TORNADO FINALLY LIFTED WITH A FEW LARGE LIMBS DOWN OFF OF CROSS ANCHOR RD. WE APPRECIATE THE ASSISTANCE FROM LAURENS COUNTY AND SPARTANBURG COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF LESLIE WAY AND SUGARLOAF RD AND TRACKED SOUTHEAST ACROSS SUGARLOAF LN AND NEAR NAOMI DR. DAMAGE WAS PRIMARILY LIMITED TO MULTIPLE UPROOTED TREES AND SNAPPED LARGE LIMBS IN THIS AREA, ALTHOUGH MINOR DAMAGE OCCURRED TO THE ROOF OF ONE HOME AND TO THE EXTERIOR OF A MANUFACTURED HOME. A COUPLE OF OTHER HOUSES WERE DAMAGED BY FALLING TREES.

NWS Storm Survey determined that a swath of damaging winds went through the Ninety Six National Historic Site Sunday evening June 25. Scattered strong, healthy trees were uprooted across the site stretching from the north end of the park from the Visitors Center to the Star Fort to the Star Fort Pond, but a burst of stronger winds estimated between 80-90mph resulted in more intense tree damage toward the southern end of the park down the Gouedy Trail, uprooting trees in the Unidentified Cemetery and along the Charleston Road towards the edge of the Park property along Paysinger Road. In all, likely well over 150 strong, healthy trees were uprooted within the park, causing as-yet unknown impacts of archeological significance. Time estimated based on radar.

Further discussion with the Pickens County emergency manager in conjunction with a plethora of social media pictures revealed that an extensive swath of wind damage occurred across the city of Pickens. Many trees were downed with a significant number falling onto power lines. Several power poles were also snapped. Pictures indicate that at least 8 homes sustained damage from falling trees, including two homes with significant damage to much of the structure. Other damage included the destruction of at least 3 outbuildings, metal highway signs bent over, and the destruction of numerous event tents.

An intense swath of straight line wind occurred across central Catawba County on the afternoon of August 7th. Peak wind speeds reached 80mph in a swath extending east from Mountain View to the Newton/Conover area. Large numbers of trees were snapped and/or uprooted with numerous large tree branches downed. Several power poles were snapped and a few structures sustained damage, including a building at the Hickory American Legion Fairgrounds.

An EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 110mph tracked across portions of Catawba, Iredell and Rowan Counties on the afternoon of August 7th. The tornado touched down between Conover and Claremont on Kelly Blvd where large tree branches were snapped. The tornado moved southeast and continued to snap large tree limbs as it neared the Murrays Hill community. The tornado began to strengthen and reached EF-1 intensity as it crossed NC Highway 10 where trees were uprooted with additional trees snapped/uprooted along Murrays Mill Rd and Sherrills Ford Rd with winds reaching 100-110mph. The tornado maintained EF-1 intensity as it passed just north of Sherrills Ford downing trees along Long Island Rd, Hopewell Church Rd and Mollys Backbone Rd. The tornado crossed Lake Norman and moved into Iredell County downing many trees along Northview Harbor Dr, Penicillin Pt and Fern Hill Rd. As the tornado neared Mooresville it began to lose intensity and weakened to an EF-0 with 70-85mph winds snapping numerous large tree branches and downing several trees across the north side of the city. The tornado briefly regained EF-1 intensity as it crossed into Rowan County along Highway 152 where 90-95mph winds uprooted/snapped numerous trees. The tornado lost intensity again as it neared Landis with 75-80mph winds snapping large limbs and downing several trees as the tornado moved through the city. The tornado dissipated just east of Interstate 85 between Lentz Rd and Daughtery Rd where mainly large limbs were downed with sporadic trees uprooted.

The NWS office in Greenville-Spartanburg has confirmed and EF0 tornado touched down just south of Lake Norman in Mecklenburg County, NC Monday evening. A very brief and weak tornado touched down Tuesday night in southwest Chester County off Leeds Rd and Breeland Dr. Damage consisted of a few downed limbs and one split tree. Path length is estimated due to inaccessibility. We conducted the storm survey based on radar indicated tornado debris signature.
